sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.64 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called a monster of the second kind\/ if it has elements of order$> 2$and if for any such element$a$and any proper subgroup$H$of$G$there exists an infinite subset${ M}_{a,H}$consisting of conjugates of$a$by elements of$G H$such that$< a,c > =G$for all$c { M}_{a,H}$.
